question: Big Bash League: Sydney Sixers vs Hobart Hurricanes (Game 1)
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Sydney Sixers and Hobart Hurricanes scheduled for January 10 2026 in Big Bash League.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50

question: Big Bash League: Sydney Sixers vs Hobart Hurricanes (Game 1) - Toss Match Double Sydney Sixers Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Sydney Sixers and Hobart Hurricanes scheduled for 2026-01-11 in Big Bash League.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Sydney Sixers will be considered correct if  Sydney Sixers is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Hobart Hurricanes will be considered correct if Hobart Hurricanes is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

question: Big Bash League: Sydney Sixers vs Hobart Hurricanes (Game 1) - Most Sixes Sydney Sixers Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Sydney Sixers and Hobart Hurricanes scheduled for 2026-01-11 in Big Bash League.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Sydney Sixers will be considered correct if Sydney Sixers is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Hobart Hurricanes.The outcome corresponding to Hobart Hurricanes will be considered correct if Hobart Hurricanes is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Sydney Sixers.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: Big Bash League: Melbourne Renegades vs Melbourne Stars (Game 1)
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Melbourne Renegades and Melbourne Stars scheduled for January 10 2026 in Big Bash League.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50

question: Big Bash League: Melbourne Renegades vs Melbourne Stars (Game 1) - Toss Match Double Melbourne Renegades Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Melbourne Renegades and Melbourne Stars scheduled for 2026-01-10 in Big Bash League.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Melbourne Renegades will be considered correct if  Melbourne Renegades is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Melbourne Stars will be considered correct if Melbourne Stars is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

description:

The outcome corresponding to Melbourne Renegades will be considered correct if Melbourne Renegades is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Melbourne Stars.The outcome corresponding to Melbourne Stars will be considered correct if Melbourne Stars is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Melbourne Renegades.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/

question: SA20: Paarl Royals vs Pretoria Capitals (Game 1) - Most Sixes Paarl Royals Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Paarl Royals and Pretoria Capitals scheduled for 2026-01-10 in SA20.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Paarl Royals will be considered correct if Paarl Royals is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Pretoria Capitals.The outcome corresponding to Pretoria Capitals will be considered correct if Pretoria Capitals is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Paarl Royals.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: Solana Up or Down - January 5, 12:00AM-4:00AM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the Solana price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than or equal to the price at the beginning of that range. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Down".
The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the SOL/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/sol-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream SOL/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Kessler vs. Keys: Total Sets O/U 2.5
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between McCartney Kessler and Madison Keys in the Brisbane International, scheduled for January 6 2026.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of sets completed equals or exceeds 3. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
A super tie breaker is considered as one (1) set for total set markets.
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official WTA statistics.

events.title: Brisbane International: McCartney Kessler vs Madison Keys
events.description:
This market refers on the tennis match between McCartney Kessler and Madison Keys in the Brisbane International, scheduled for January 5 at 7:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Kessler' if McCartney Kessler advances against Madison Keys.

This market will resolve to 'Keys' if Madison Keys advances against McCartney Kessler.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

description:

This market will resolve to "Olympique de Marseille" if Olympique de Marseille win the game by 2 or more goals.

question: XRP Up or Down - January 6, 1AM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the XRP/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the XRP/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/XRP_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance XRP/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

This market refers on the tennis match between Karolina Muchova and Ajla Tomljanovic in the Brisbane International, scheduled for January 5 at 7:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Muchova' if Karolina Muchova advances against Ajla Tomljanovic.

This market will resolve to 'Tomljanovic' if Ajla Tomljanovic advances against Karolina Muchova.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.
